scope_boundary: Explicit v1.0 no-promotion decisions for manifest chapter core claims that do not yet have accepted evidence-transition records. These decisions keep chapter core support states at argument and do not promote any ASI capability, runtime behavior, benchmark, safety, deployment, source-interpretation, or model-quality claim.
reviewer_independence: local maintainer-agent review only; not an independent external audit
global_promotion_burden: Move a chapter core claim above argument only through an accepted evidence-transition record that names the narrowed claim, reviewed artifacts, commands where relevant, limitations, counterevidence, non-claims, and support-state effect.

decisions.chapter_title: ASI Is a Stack, Not a Model
decisions.decision_reason: The chapter has source mappings and finite stack-boundary Lean hooks, but no end-to-end stack implementation, deployed handoff trace, integration benchmark, or independent architecture review has been accepted as evidence for the broad stack claim.
required_evidence: layer handoff artifacts with authority receipts
accepted transition for a narrowed stack-mechanics claim
blockers: no deployed or replayed stack run
finite proofs do not establish whole-stack behavior
non_claims: does not prove ASI is implemented
does not prove stack architecture outperforms model-centric alternatives

decisions.chapter_title: Failure Modes of Ungoverned Intelligence
decisions.decision_reason: The chapter organizes failure modes and carries finite promotion-blocking proof hooks, but no adversarial system run, incident corpus analysis, or failure-detection benchmark has been accepted for the broad failure taxonomy.
required_evidence: replayed failure traces or incident mapping
accepted transition for a bounded failure detector or taxonomy claim
blockers: no deployed monitor or detector trace
non_claims: does not prove catastrophic-risk coverage
does not prove deployed failure detection

decisions.chapter_title: Human Intent as a Formal Input
decisions.decision_reason: The chapter has exact source mappings and finite intent-contract hooks, but no parser evaluation, user-study evidence, contract compiler run, or intent-to-execution trace has been accepted for support-state promotion.
non_claims: does not prove intent capture quality
does not prove human-goal preservation

decisions.chapter_title: Cognitive Compilation and Semantic IR
decisions.decision_reason: The chapter has source mappings and finite obligation-preservation hooks, but no compiler implementation, lowering trace, repair benchmark, or semantic-equivalence review has been accepted.
non_claims: does not prove cognitive compiler correctness
does not prove artifact quality improvement

decisions.chapter_title: Context Transactions, Snapshots, Mounts, and Taint
decisions.decision_reason: The chapter has source mappings, finite transaction hooks, synthetic context-admission deletion-closure fixtures, finite materialization/deletion-closure route proofs, and a bounded synthetic memory-store harness, but no deployed memory-store implementation, deployed taint propagation run, deployed deletion-closure verification, side-channel review, or branch-isolation replay has been accepted.
blockers: finite deletion-closure and bounded synthetic memory-store fixture/proof coverage only
no deployed deletion-closure, side-channel, or branch-isolation review
non_claims: does not prove context memory correctness
does not prove taint isolation in a deployed system

decisions.chapter_title: Verification Bandwidth and Context Adequacy
decisions.decision_reason: The chapter has source mappings and finite adequacy hooks, but no contradiction-rate result, distractor-resistance test, adequacy classifier evaluation, or verification-bandwidth benchmark has been accepted.
non_claims: does not prove context adequacy detection
does not prove source interpretation quality

decisions.chapter_title: Labor OS and Typed Jobs
decisions.decision_reason: The chapter has source mappings and finite job-lifecycle hooks, but no Labor OS runner, approval-service trace, adapter execution log, or job-delivery replay has been accepted.

does not prove job scheduling behavior

decisions.chapter_title: Artifact Graphs, Audit Logs, and Replay

non_claims: does not prove replayability of real work
does not prove audit-log completeness

decisions.chapter_title: Procedural Memory and Cognitive Loop Closure
decisions.decision_reason: The chapter has source mappings and finite procedure-card hooks, but no trace-mining run, synthesized reusable tool, regression suite, or routed procedural-memory result has been accepted.

does not prove safe tool promotion

decisions.chapter_title: Routing Heads and Specialist Cores
decisions.decision_reason: The chapter has source mappings, finite routing hooks, and a folded MoECOT runtime-crosswalk hook set, but no router run, specialist-core benchmark, runtime replay, orchestration artifact, fallback trace, or route-quality measurement has been accepted.

blockers: no imported MoECOT orchestration benchmark or replay artifact
non_claims: does not prove routing efficiency
does not prove specialist-core quality
does not prove MoECOT runtime behavior
does not prove low-parameter ASI performance

does not prove safe rented-compute execution

decisions.chapter_title: Compact Generative Systems: Generate, Verify, Repair, and Residual Honesty
decisions.decision_reason: The merged chapter has architecture, source mappings, record-shape fixtures, and finite Lean predicates for compact generation and generate/verify/repair receipts, but no compact-generation backend, codec, reconstruction benchmark, repair-cost measurement, residual-honesty benchmark, or model/artifact run has been accepted.
non_claims: does not prove compact generation quality
does not prove efficiency gains
does not prove compression quality
does not prove repair loop convergence

decisions.chapter_title: RankFold, NeuralFold, and Artifact Compression

does not prove artifact compression preserves utility

decisions.chapter_title: Mathematical and Search Substrates
decisions.decision_reason: The chapter has source mappings and finite substrate-adoption hooks, but no baseline-symmetric search, routing, or compression measurement has been accepted for optional mathematical substrates.
non_claims: does not prove geometric or cyclic substrates improve search
does not make any substrate core to the stack

decisions.chapter_title: Circle Calculus and Proof-Carrying AI Contracts
decisions.decision_reason: The chapter has local-project source mappings, finite proof-contract hooks, and one accepted narrow external Circle rope receipt replay, but no chapter-core Circle receipt bundle, ASI Stack consumer-gate run, workload baseline, or independent theorem-package review has been accepted.
required_evidence: public-safe Circle receipt bundle tied to the chapter core claim
blockers: narrow external rope receipt replay is not a chapter-core replay bundle
no workload metric tied to contract use
non_claims: does not prove Circle improves AI behavior
does not treat structural proof as model-quality evidence

decisions.chapter_title: Coil Attention, Cyclic Memory, and Recurrence Contracts
decisions.decision_reason: The chapter has source mappings and finite coil-memory hooks, but no cyclic-memory implementation, retrieval-quality benchmark, recurrence-control replay, or alias-risk measurement has been accepted.
required_evidence: retrieval-quality benchmark separated from structural facts
non_claims: does not prove retrieval quality
does not prove reasoning quality from sparse coverage facts

decisions.chapter_title: CoilRA, MultiCoil RoPE, and Cyclic Mixers
decisions.decision_reason: The chapter has source mappings and finite cyclic-mixer hooks, but no dense, LoRA, RoPE, learned, recurrent, or state-space baseline comparison has been accepted.
non_claims: does not prove cyclic mixers outperform baselines
does not promote structural guarantees into quality claims

decisions.chapter_title: Policy Optimization and Learning from Feedback
decisions.decision_reason: The chapter has extensive external-literature source notes and finite policy-update hooks, but no local training run, policy update, reward-hacking probe, holdout evaluation, or governed deployment canary has been accepted.
non_claims: does not prove any RL or preference method improves the stack
does not prove reward safety

decisions.chapter_title: Data Engines, Continual Learning, and Unlearning
decisions.decision_reason: The chapter has four reviewed source notes plus a finite public-safe receipt schema and admission-routing proof, but no ASI Stack data-admission runtime, dataset import, training run, continual-learning comparison, contamination measurement, deletion propagation trace, or independently replayed evaluation.
required_evidence: public-safe workload with frozen baseline and declared data versions
heldout exact and semantic contamination controls plus negative cases
workload-scoped replacement, accumulation, replay, quarantine, retraining, and residual comparison
descendant-deletion scope report with independently replayed verification
blockers: no local data engine or dataset admission runtime
no measured data quality, coverage, model quality, privacy, or synthetic-data result
no verified deletion across checkpoints, adapters, caches, retrieval stores, distilled models, or published artifacts
non_claims: does not prove data quality or contamination absence
does not prove synthetic-data safety or model-collapse avoidance
does not prove machine unlearning, privacy compliance, or verified forgetting
does not promote any learning, benchmark, safety, or capability claim

decisions.chapter_title: Artifact Steward Agents and Living Project Governance
decisions.decision_reason: The chapter has source mappings, schemas, fixtures, and finite steward-agent hooks, but no steward bot, governance run, treasury action, contribution-ledger trace, or release-gate execution has been accepted.
blockers: no release-gate execution over a real artifact
non_claims: does not prove artifact stewardship works
does not grant autonomous governance authority

decisions.chapter_title: Integrated Reference Architecture
decisions.decision_reason: The chapter integrates the architecture and has finite reference-trace hooks, but no end-to-end reference trace, cross-layer runtime replay, architecture red-team, or external review has been accepted.
non_claims: does not prove integrated ASI behavior
does not prove cross-layer safety

decisions.chapter_title: Project Theseus as Report-First Implementation Reference
decisions.decision_reason: The chapter has local-project source mappings, finite report-contract hooks, and one public-safe static architecture-gate report import checked by digest, but no clean live Theseus report bundle, accepted gate replay, benchmark receipt, or public-safe end-to-end implementation trace has been imported.
required_evidence: clean public-safe Theseus report bundle or archived release fixture
blockers: static architecture-gate import exists only as implementation-reference evidence
non_claims: does not promote private Theseus results
does not prove general ASI capability

decisions.chapter_title: Prototype Roadmap

non_claims: does not prove the prototype exists
does not prove roadmap phases are complete

decisions.chapter_title: Adversarial Evaluation, Sandbagging, and Training-Time Deception
decisions.decision_reason: The chapter has source-noted threat-model comparators and a finite promotion-blocking theorem, but no local deceptive-model evaluation, integrity-record fixture, detector, mitigation, cross-context workload, or independent replay has been accepted as evidence for the broad claim.
required_evidence: public-safe evaluation-integrity record with negative controls
cross-context workload with held-out controls and independent replay
accepted transition for a narrowed evaluation-integrity claim
blockers: no local deceptive-model or detector result
no cross-context workload or independent replay
finite theorem does not establish sandbagging resistance or evaluator correctness
non_claims: does not prove a model is deceptive
does not prove resistance to sandbagging or reward hacking
does not prove evaluator correctness or safety

decisions.chapter_title: Open-Ended Improvement Engines
decisions.decision_reason: The chapter has source-noted comparators and a finite admission-routing theorem, but no local improvement campaign, task generator, candidate archive, evaluator, independent review, resource measurement, stop-boundary fixture, or replay has been accepted as evidence for the broad claim.
required_evidence: public-safe improvement-campaign record with negative controls
independent evaluation and archive/residual replay for a bounded workload
accepted transition for a narrowed campaign-admission claim
blockers: no local generation campaign or candidate archive
finite theorem does not establish discovery quality, evaluator correctness, safe self-improvement, or generality
non_claims: does not prove autonomous discovery or R&D
does not prove safe or open-ended self-improvement
does not prove evaluator correctness, model quality, safety, or ASI

decisions.chapter_title: Governed Deliberation and Test-Time Scaling
decisions.decision_reason: The chapter has source-noted deliberation comparators and finite routing theorems, but no local reasoning model, search, verifier, test-time budget policy, workload, independent review, execution trace, or measured result has been accepted as evidence for the broad claim.

accepted transition for a narrowed deliberation-admission claim

finite theorems do not establish verifier correctness, reasoning quality, safety, or generality

decisions.chapter_title: Model-Weight Custody and Hardware Roots of Trust
decisions.decision_reason: The chapter has source-noted model-weight security, confidential-computing, vendor lifecycle, and release-scheme comparators plus a finite invalid-attestation load route, but no ASI Stack weight store, encrypted artifact, TEE, key service, attestation, custody audit, extraction test, release decision, readiness decision, or deployment result has been accepted as evidence for the broad claim.
required_evidence: public-safe synthetic custody workload with bounded valid and stale/invalid-attestation, changed-policy, widened-scope, release, revocation, and lineage controls
independent review of a bounded custody threat model, key-release policy, measurement/verifier scope, and release residual record
accepted transition for a narrowed custody-record or key-release-routing claim
blockers: no local weight store, encrypted artifact, key service, TEE, hardware attestation, custody audit, or model load
no adversarial security assessment, extraction test, incident exercise, or release-policy evaluation
finite theorems do not establish hardware trustworthiness, confidentiality, extraction resistance, model safety, readiness, authority, or generality
non_claims: does not prove that any weight is protected, confidential, secure, or resistant to extraction
does not prove hardware, vendor, operator, software, key-service, or attestation trustworthiness
does not prove model safety, release desirability, readiness, authority, deployment, or ASI

decisions.chapter_title: AI Supply-Chain Integrity and Lifecycle Provenance
decisions.decision_reason: The chapter has source-noted C-SCRM, build-provenance, model-signing, and AI BOM comparators plus finite advisory/signature quarantine routes, but no ASI Stack AI BOM, supplier assessment, build or training provenance, signing/verification, advisory feed, derivative graph, revocation action, reproducible rebuild, incident exercise, readiness decision, or deployment result has been accepted as evidence for the broad claim.
required_evidence: public-safe synthetic supply-chain workload with bounded valid, missing-lineage, stale-BOM, unverified-signature, unresolved-critical-advisory, revoked-signer, changed-derivative, revocation, and residual controls
independent review of a bounded supply-chain threat model, provenance/signature verification policy, inventory freshness, advisory-routing, and affected-release record
accepted transition for a narrowed supply-chain admission-routing or re-review-propagation claim
blockers: no local AI BOM, supplier assessment, build or training provenance, signer, verifier, advisory feed, derivative graph, revocation action, or model artifact admission
no reproducible rebuild, supplier review, incident exercise, vulnerability/advisory evaluation, or inventory-completeness assessment
finite theorems do not establish artifact correctness, absence of compromise, data fitness, trustworthy suppliers, legal compliance, model safety, readiness, authority, or generality
non_claims: does not prove complete lineage, an AI BOM, artifact integrity, absence of compromise, or trustworthy suppliers
does not prove data fitness, legal compliance, secure training/build behavior, signature effectiveness, or effective revocation
does not prove model safety, readiness, authority, deployment, or ASI

does not prove model quality, safety, or ASI
